notes: Durham County Council site investigation at Framwellgate Bridge, Durham; Drg. No. 1947, July 1963; 'SECTION OF BORING D.1'. Scan title spells the place 'FRAMWELLGATE' (the task index gives 'FRANWELLGATE'); boring labelled 'D.1' on the scan. IMPERIAL log: single depth-from-surface scale in feet & inches (scale 5 ft to 1 in); no fathoms column, so fathom slot recorded as 0 and full feet placed in the feet slot; thickness is depth[i]-depth[i-1] (no separate thickness column to cross-check, so no clerk arithmetic slips possible). Surface datum written '101.00 A.O.D.' = 101.00 ft AOD (=30.8 m; a metric reading of 101 m is geologically impossible at this riverside site). Sequence: made ground 0-16'6", glacial drift (sandy/silty clays with stones + brown sands) to 51'0", then Coal Measures mudstone/siltstone/sandstone to TD 118'0". NO coal seam is logged in the section (the 'coal' at 28'6" and 34'0" is drift clast material), hence seams/seam_marks empty. Faint pencil marginalia '101 / 92 / 49' beside the ~52 ft rockhead is a reduced-level jotting by a later hand (101 AOD surface, ~49 ft AOD rockhead), NOT a seam correlation. Faintness: whole log is a low-contrast photocopy with a BGS watermark; boundaries were cross-checked by pixel-scale line detection. The 92'0"-92'6"-94'0" cluster and the thin dark-grey-shale marker are the least legible (marked 'probable'); half-foot readings at 51'9"/52'6" and 63'5" verified by zoom. Core-run graduation lines at 94'0", 97'6", 111'0" are NOT lithology boundaries and were folded into their host units (21 lithological intervals total). Final depth 118'0" = 35.97 m vs index depth_m 36.27 m (0.30 m / ~1 ft discrepancy, within tolerance; scan clearly reads 118'0"). Page 2 is an Ordnance Survey location plan of central Durham with the borehole circled ('BOREHOLE'), no strata.
